title: Concurrency in Python: GIL, asyncio, multiprocessing + DSA Heap finish
focus: When threads help, when they don't; futures and queues for ML pipelines.

items.label: Python GIL — what it does and doesn't lock
interviewQuestions: Explain the GIL and why threading helps I/O-bound but not CPU-bound work in CPython.
When does multiprocessing beat threading for ML workloads?
What does the GIL protect in CPython, and why is reference counting part of the reason it exists?

items.label: asyncio: event loop, coroutines, gather
interviewQuestions: When is asyncio the right choice over threads/processes?
What are common pitfalls with mixing sync and async code?
Compare coroutines vs OS threads: who controls context switching, and why does that matter?
What makes a function blocking vs non-blocking, and how would you isolate blocking work inside an async service?

items.label: Producer-consumer, queues, futures
interviewQuestions: How would you parallelize an embarrassingly parallel inference workload?
Compare ThreadPoolExecutor vs ProcessPoolExecutor — when does each fit, and what does the GIL mean here?
What does as_completed give you that executor.map does not?
Why might multiprocessing still need locks or a Manager-backed dictionary?
